transcript.summary: User asks for Claude Code support alongside Codex.
transcript.content: Continue developing TraceForge. Add a Claude Code adapter path alongside Codex, normalize both fixture types, and verify them locally.

transcript.summary: Assistant outlines the dual-adapter plan and inspects the workspace.

content.text: I’ll add a Claude Code adapter boundary, teach the importer to detect both formats, and update the docs before validating both fixtures.

input.command: find packages fixtures docs -maxdepth 4 -type f | sort

transcript.summary: Bash tool returns the importer and docs layout.
Assistant creates the Claude adapter file.

content.text: Next I’m adding a dedicated Claude Code adapter module so importer logic stops assuming Codex-only inputs.

transcript.summary: Write tool confirms the new adapter file.
Importer and docs are updated for dual-adapter support.
transcript.diff_summary: Added Claude Code detection, dual-fixture docs, and validation notes.

transcript.summary: Assistant validates both fixtures through the ingest flow.

content.text: I’m validating both fixture paths through the importer and CLI ingest flow so the normalized event model stays shared.

input.command: node packages/cli/src/main.js ingest fixtures/claude-code-session.json /tmp/claude.json && node packages/cli/src/main.js ingest fixtures/codex-session.json /tmp/codex.json

transcript.summary: Validation command passes for both fixtures.

content.stdout:
Ingested 9 events from fixtures/claude-code-session.json
Wrote normalized session JSON to /tmp/claude.json
Ingested 9 events from fixtures/codex-session.json
Wrote normalized session JSON to /tmp/codex.json

transcript.result_summary: TraceForge now detects and normalizes both Codex and Claude Code session fixtures into the shared event model.
